name: Hyperbola-1

overview.value: Hyperbola-1 (aka Shuangquxian-1, SQX-1) is a Chinese four-stage, all-solid-fuel rocket guided by liquid-fuel attitude control engines, 20.8 m tall and 1.4 m in diameter, weighing 31 t. It is manufactured by i-Space (Beijing Interstellar Glory Space Technology Co., Ltd.) and its current status is active.

base.reason: established aggregator (Gunter's Space Page), flight-by-flight table cross-check confirms date (weekly maintenance 2026-07-13)

notes: Added 2026-07-08 per Florian (verified during the backfill, previously skipped as off the phase-3 authorized list). Weekly maintenance 2026-07-13: LL2 rate-limited this run (429 on first call, stopped per budget rule); cross-checked instead against Gunter's Space Page's flight-by-flight table, which independently confirms the prior provisional first_flight_date/flights_total/flights_successful/last_flight_date exactly, so those four fields are upgraded from provisional (china-in-space.com, tier 3) to canonical (Gunter's, tier 4); height_m/diameter_m/mass_kg added from the same Gunter's page (Hyperbola-1(1) base variant figures). CONFLICT for Florian: payload_leo_kg is 520 kg per Wikipedia (currently on file, unscored/canonical) vs 300 kg per Gunter's Space Page (canonical aggregator) for the Hyperbola-1(1) variant; not overwritten, needs reconciliation. vehicle_class and reusable remain null: no fetched page states them explicitly (Gunter's lists no reusability field, an omission, not a stated 'expendable').

events.headline: Hyperbola-1(2) fails to reach orbit on second flight
Hyperbola-1(2) fails to reach orbit on third flight
Hyperbola-1(2) fails on fourth flight, a third straight failure
Hyperbola-1(2) returns to flight with successful dummy payload launch
